title: fagara quassiifolia
intro: Fagara quassiifolia, also known as Chinese wax tree or Quassia amara, is a type of plant that belongs to the family Simaroubaceae. It is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to tropical and subtropical regions of Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral effects. Fagara quassiifolia has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and digestive problems, and is often prepared as a tea, decoction, or extract, which can also be used topically to relieve skin irritations and wounds.
